column1: ity. p11 peptide was encapsulated in poly(lactic-co-glycolic acid) (PLGA) nanoparticles and
embedded into an injectable polyethylene glycol-polycaprolactone-polyethylene glycol
(PEG-PCL-PEG)-based thermogel system (nano-thermogel) for the proposed purpose of
introduction into and gel formation in the eye, following injection either in the subconjunc-
tival space or intravitreally (Figure 1). PLGA, a FDA-approved biodegradable polymer,
has been applied for the delivery of various therapeutic agents in the eye and is generally
well tolerated in vivo [20–22]. Moreover, several studies have been reported where PLGA
nanoparticles mediated efﬁcient and stable delivery of peptide molecules [23,24].
